main.title:
United Airlines down more than 3%
after forcibly removing a passenger
main.text: Today the governing council of the Bank of Canada will publish their decision  on the country’s key interest rate. Traders are watching hawkishly - this piece of economic data is the determining factor in currency valuation.

extra.text-s: All things considered, this event is a major market mover to watch for!

content.title: United Airlines down more than 3% after forcibly removing a passenger
content.subtitle:
Increased profitability
on USD/JPY
content.text-1: United Continental stock lost more than 3.50% in pre-market trading after a disturbing video of them violently removing a passenger from a flight went viral.
content.text-2:
United staff was forced pick a passenger at random to be removed from the aircraft due to the flight being overbooked. The passenger refused to step off and was violently pulled by airport police officers, causing him to fall and injure his head. Bloodied and disoriented the passenger was mumbling one phrase repeatedly: “I have to go home”.

Naturally, most market participants would assume that UAL stock would continue to decline after the U.S. markets open. But while that’s a very real possibility, there’s also a chance some would use this opportunity to buy the stock while it’s cheap.

Do you think United stock will take a nosedive or drift up? Make your own prediction!
content.text-3: Turn this occasion into a trading opportunity with special 92%* profitability on USD/JPY!
